name: Test Coverage Report
description: Runs the project's coverage tooling, summarizes coverage by module, identifies the riskiest under-tested areas, and proposes concrete test cases to close the most important gaps.
author: ClaudeStudio Code Quality Pack

inputs.label: Coverage command
inputs.help: Command that runs the test suite with coverage, e.g. 'npm run test:coverage', 'pytest --cov', 'cargo tarpaulin'.

inputs.label: Coverage output file (optional)
inputs.help: Path to a machine-readable report (lcov.info, coverage.xml, coverage-final.json) if the command emits one.

steps.title: Run coverage
steps.prompt: Run the coverage command `{{coverage_cmd}}` from the repository root. Capture the summary output. If it fails, surface the error and stop before reporting. Note the overall line and branch coverage.

steps.title: Analyze gaps
steps.prompt: Parse the coverage results (use {{coverage_file}} if provided, otherwise the command output). Build a per-module/per-file table of line and branch coverage. Cross-reference low-coverage files with their apparent importance (core logic, error handling, security-relevant code, recently changed files) to identify the highest-risk gaps relative to the {{target_pct}}% target.

steps.title: Report & suggested tests
steps.prompt: Write a Markdown report: 'Overall Coverage' (line/branch vs {{target_pct}}% target with pass/fail), 'Coverage by Module' (table), 'Highest-Risk Gaps' (ranked), and 'Suggested Test Cases' — for the top gaps, list concrete test cases (function, scenario, expected behavior, edge cases) a developer can implement directly. Do not write test files; only propose them.
